## Step 1: Use the general solution for $\sin a \sin b = c \sin (a + b) + d \sin (a - b)$
We are given $(\sin x)^7 = a \sin 7x + b \sin 5x + c \sin 3x + d \sin x$. Substituting the result into the original equation, we get $\cos 9x \sin x = c \sin 12x + d \sin 2x$.
